question.text: For transfer of shares from a Non-Resident to a Resident in unlisted companies under FEMA, the negotiated price shall not be more than:

answer.text: The minimum price at which transfer can be made from a resident to a non-resident
The fair market value as determined by a SEBI registered Merchant Banker
The book value of shares as per the latest audited balance sheet
The price determined by the Reserve Bank of India

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For transfer of existing shares by Non-resident to a Resident, the negotiated price shall not be more than the minimum price at which the transfer of shares can be made from a resident to a non-resident. This creates a pricing ceiling for NR-to-Resident transfers that mirrors the floor for Resident-to-NR transfers. **The Trap:** For Resident to Non-Resident transfers in unlisted companies, the price shall not be less than fair value (floor price). For NR to Resident, the price shall not be more than this floor (ceiling price). The direction of the pricing limit changes. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This asymmetric pricing ensures that foreign investors don't sell shares at inflated prices to residents and protects against capital flight concerns.
